result.stdout:
Get a list of pull requests in a git repository.

Usage: devops git pull-request list [options]

Options:
  -h|--help          Show help information.
  --repository       The id or friendly name of the repository.
  --top              Number of pull requests to get.
                     Default value is: 100.
  --skip             Number of pull requests to skip.
                     Default value is: 0.
  --timerange-type   The type of time range on which the pull requests are
                     filtered.
                     Allowed values are: Created, Closed.
                     Default value is: Created.
  --min-time         If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed
                     after this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.
  --max-time         If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed
                     before this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.
  --status           The status of the pull request.
                     Allowed values are: NotSet, Active, Completed, Abandoned,
                     All.
                     Default value is: NotSet.
  --export-all       If specified, retrieves and exports all available pull
                     requests.
  --output-file      File to export the pullrequest list. If this value is not
                     provided the output will be the console. If --export-all is
                     set this --output-file is required.
  -p|--project       Tfs project name
  -u|--service-url   URL to the service you will connect to, e.g.
                     https://dev.azure.com/organization.
  -t|--token         Personal access token.
  --non-interactive  When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

result.stdout:
Resolve legacy identity information for use with older APIs such as the Security APIs.

Usage: devops identity list [options]

Options:
  -h|--help           Show help information.
  --identity-ids      Specify a non-default scope (collection, project) to
                      search for groups.
  --query-membership  The membership information to include with the identities.
                      Values can be None for no membership data or Direct to
                      include the groups that the identity is a member of and
                      the identities that are a member of this identity (groups
                      only).
                      Allowed values are: None, Direct, Expanded, ExpandedDown,
                      ExpandedUp.
                      Default value is: None.
  --search-filter     The type of search to perform. Values can be AccountName
                      (domainlias), DisplayName, MailAddress, General (display
                      name, account name, or unique name), or LocalGroupName
                      (only search Azure Devops groups).
  --filter-value      The search value, as specified by the searchFilter.
  -u|--service-url    URL to the service you will connect to, e.g.
                      https://dev.azure.com/organization.
  -t|--token          Personal access token.
  --non-interactive   When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.
